book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
The Captions of the Arabic Canons Attributed to the Council of Nice.
title: The Captions of the Arabic Canons Attributed to the Council of Nice.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Insane persons and energumens should not be ordained.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Bond servants are not to be ordained.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Neophytes in the faith are not to be ordained to Holy Orders before they have a knowledge of Holy Scripture.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And such, if convicted after their ordination of grave sin, are to be deposed with those who ordained them.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: The cohabitation of women with bishops, presbyters, and deacons prohibited on account of their celibacy.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: We decree that bishops shall not live with women; nor shall a presbyter who is a widower; neither shall they escort them; nor be familiar with them, nor gaze upon them persistently.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And the same decree is made with regard to every celibate priest, and the same concerning such deacons as have no wives.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And this is to be the case whether the woman be beautiful or ugly, whether a young girl or beyond the age of puberty, whether great in birth, or an orphan taken out of charity under pretext of bringing her up.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: For the devil with such arms slays religious, bishops, presbyters, and deacons, and incites them to the fires of desire.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: But if she be an old woman, and of advanced age, or a sister, or mother, or aunt, or grandmother, it is permitted to live with these because such persons are free from all suspicion of scandal.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the election of a bishop and of the confirmation of the election.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: That those excommunicated by one bishop are not to be received by another; and that those whose excommunication has been shown to have been unjust should be absolved by the archbishop or patriarch.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: That provincial Councils should be held twice a year, for the consideration of all things affecting the churches of the bishops of the province.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the patriarchs of Alexandria and Antioch, and of their jurisdiction.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of one who solicits the episcopate when the people do not wish him; or if they do desire him, but without the consent of the archbishop.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: How the bishop of Jerusalem is to be honoured, the honour, however, of the metropolitan church of Cæsarea being preserved intact, to which he is subject.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of those who force themselves into the order of presbyters without election or examination.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the bishop who ordains one whom he understands has denied the faith; also of one ordained who after that he had denied it, crept into orders.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of one who of his own will goes to another church, having been chosen by it, and does not wish afterwards to stay there.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of taking pains that he be transferred from his own church to another.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: No one shall become a monk without the bishop’s license, and why a license is required.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: That clerics or religious who lend on usury should be cast from their grade.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the honour to be paid to the bishop and to a presbyter by the deacons.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the system and of the manner of receiving those who are converted from the heresy of Paul of Samosata.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the system and manner of receiving those who are converted from the heresy the Novatians.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the system and manner of receiving those who return after a lapse from the faith, and of receiving the relapsed, and of those brought into peril of death by sickness before their penance is finished, and concerning such as are convalescent.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of avoiding the conversation of evil workers and wizards, also of the penance of them that have not avoided such.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of incestuous marriages contrary to the law of spiritual relationship, and of the penance of such as are in such marriages.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of sponsors in baptism.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Men shall not hold females at the font, neither women males; but women females, and men males.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the prohibited marriages of spiritual brothers and sisters from receiving them in baptism.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of him who has married two wives at the same time, or who through lust has added another woman to his wife; and of his punishment.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Part of the canon.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: If he be a priest he is forbidden to sacrifice and is cut off from the communion of the faithful until he turn out of the house the second woman, and he ought to retain the first.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: That no one should be forbidden Holy Communion unless such as are doing penance.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Clerics are forbidden from suretyship or witness-giving in criminal causes.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of avoiding the excommunicate, and of not receiving the oblation from them; and of the excommunication of him who does not avoid the excommunicated.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: How anger, indignation, and hatred should be avoided by the priest, especially because he has the power of excommunicating others.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of not kneeling in prayer.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of giving [only] names of Christians in baptism, and of heretics who retain the faith in the Trinity and the perfect form of baptism; and of others not retaining it, worthy of a worse name, and of how such are to be received when they come to the faith.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the system and manner of receiving converts to the Orthodox faith from the heresy of Arius and of other like.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the system of receiving those who have kept the dogmas of the faith and the Church’s laws, and yet have separated from us and afterwards come back.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the place of residence of the Patriarch, and of the honour which should be given to the bishop of Jerusalem and to the bishop of Seleucia.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the honour to be given to the Archbishop of Seleucia in the Synod of Greece.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of not holding a provincial synod in the province of Persia without the authority of the patriarch of Antioch, and how the bishops of Persia are subject to the metropolitans of Antioch.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the creation of a patriarch for Ethiopia, and of his power, and of the honour to be paid him in the Synod of Greece.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the election of the Archbishop of Cyprus, who is subject to the patriarch of Antioch.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: That the ordination of ministers of the Church by bishops in the dioceses of strangers is forbidden.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the care and power which a Patriarch has over the bishops and archbishops of his patriarchate; and of the primacy of the Bishop of Rome over all.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Let the patriarch consider what things are done by the archbishops and bishops in their provinces; and if he shall find anything done by them otherwise than it should be, let him change it, and order it, as seemeth him fit: for he is the father of all, and they are his sons.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And although the archbishop be among the bishops as an elder brother, who hath the care of his brethren, and to whom they owe obedience because he is over them; yet the patriarch is to all those who are under his power, just as he who holds the seat of Rome, is the head and prince of all patriarchs; inasmuch as he is first, as was Peter, to whom power is given over all Christian princes, and over all their peoples, as he who is the Vicar of Christ our Lord over all peoples and over the whole Christian Church, and whoever shall contradict this, is excommunicated by the Synod.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Let there be only four patriarchs in the whole world as there are four writers of the Gospel, and four rivers, etc. And let there be a prince and chief over them, the lord of the see of the Divine Peter at Rome, according as the Apostles commanded.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And after him the lord of the great Alexandria, which is the see of Mark.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And the third is the lord of Ephesus, which is the see of John the Divine who speaks divine things.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And the fourth and last is my lord of Antioch, which is another see of Peter.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And let all the bishops be divided under the hands of these four patriarchs; and the bishops of the little towns which are under the dominion of the great cities let them be under the authority of these metropolitans.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: But let every metropolitan of these great cities appoint the bishops of his province, but let none of the bishops appoint him, for he is greater than they.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Therefore let every man know his own rank, and let him not usurp the rank of another.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And whosoever shall contradict this law which we have established the Fathers of the Synod subject him to anathema.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the provincial synod which should be held twice every year, and of its utility; together with the excommunication of such as oppose the decree.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the synod of Archbishops, which meets once a year with the Patriarch, and of its utility; also of the collection to be made for the support of the patriarch throughout the provinces and places subject to the patriarch.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of a cleric or monk who when fallen into sin, and summoned once, twice, and thrice, does not present himself for trial.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: What the patriarch should do in the case of a defendant set at liberty unpunished by the decision of the bishop, presbyter, or even of a deacon, as the case may be.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: How an archbishop ought to give trial to one of his suffragan bishops.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the receiving of complaints and condemnation of an archbishop against his patriarch.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: How a patriarch should admit a complaint; or judgment of an Archbishop against an Archbishop.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of those excommunicated by a certain one, when they can be and when they cannot be absolved by another.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: No bishop shall choose his own successor.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: No simoniacal ordinations shall be made.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: There shall be but one bishop of one city, and one parochus of one town; also the incumbent, whether bishop or parish priest, shall not be removed in favour of a successor desired by some of the people unless he has been convicted of manifest crime.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Bishops shall not allow the separation of a wife from her husband on account of discord—[in American, “incompatibility of temper”].

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Usury and the base seeking of worldly gain is forbidden to the clergy, also conversation and fellowship with Jews.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Marriages with infidels to be avoided.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the election of a chorepiscopus, and of his duties in towns, and villages, and monasteries.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: How a chorepiscopus should visit the churches and monasteries which are under his jurisdiction.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of how the presbyters of the towns and villages should go twice a year with their chorepiscopus to salute the bishop, and how religious should do so once a year from their monasteries, and how the new abbot of a monastery should go thrice.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the rank in sitting during the celebration of service in church by the bishop, the archdeacon and the chorepiscopus; and of the office of archdeacon, and of the honour due the archpresbyter.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the honour due the archdeacon and the chorepiscopus when they sit in church during the absence of the bishop, and when they go about with the bishop.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: How all the grades of the clergy and their duties should be publicly described and set forth.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of how men are to be chosen from the diocese for holy orders, and of how they should be examined.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the honour due to the deacons, and how the clerics must not put themselves in their way.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: The number of presbyters and deacons is to be adapted to the work of the church and to its means.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the Ecclesiastical Economist and of the others who with him care for the church’s possessions.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the offices said in the church, the night and day offices, and of the collect for all those who rule that church.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the order to be observed at the funeral of a bishop, of a chorepiscopus and of an archdeacon, and of the office of exequies.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of taking a second wife, after the former one has been disowned for any cause, or even not put away, and of him who falsely accuses his wife of adultery.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: If any priest or deacon shall put away his wife on account of her fornication, or for other cause, as aforesaid, or cast her out of doors for external good, or that he may change her for another more beautiful, or better, or richer, or does so out of his lust which is displeasing to God; and after she has been put away for any of these causes he shall contract matrimony with another, or without having put her away shall take another, whether free or bond; and shall have both equally, they living separately and he sleeping every night with one or other of them, or else keeping both in the same house and bed, let him be deposed.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: If he were a layman let him be deprived of communion.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: But if anyone falsely defames his wife charging her with adultery, so that he turns her out of doors, the matter must be diligently examined; and if the accusation was false, he shall be deposed if a cleric, but if a layman shall be prohibited from entering the church and from the communion of the faithful; and shall be compelled to live with her whom he has defamed, even though she be deformed, and poor, and insane; and whoever shall not obey is excommunicated by the Synod.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Whatever presbyter or deacon shall put away his wife without the offence of fornication, or for any other cause of which we have spoken above, and shall cast her out of doors…such a person shall be cast out of the clergy, if he were a clergyman; if a layman he shall be forbidden the communion of the faithful.…But if that woman [untruly charged by her husband with adultery], that is to say his wife, spurns his society on account of the injury he has done her and the charge he has brought against her, of which she is innocent, let her freely be put away and let a bill of repudiation be written for her, noting the false accusation which had been brought against her.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And then if she should wish to marry some other faithful man, it is right for her to do so, nor does the Church forbid it; and the same permission extends as well to men as to women, since there is equal reason for it for each.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: But if he shall return to better fruit which is of the same kind, and shall conciliate to himself the love and benevolence of his consort, and shall be willing to return to his pristine friendship, his fault shall be condoned to him after he has done suitable and sufficient penance.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: And whoever shall speak against this decree the fathers of the synod excommunicate him.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of having two wives at the same time, and of a woman who is one of the faithful marrying an infidel; and of the form of receiving her to penance.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of giving in marriage to an infidel a daughter or sister without her knowledge and contrary to her wish.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of one of the faithful who departs from the faith through lust and love of an infidel; and of the form of receiving him back, or admitting him to penance.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the hospital to be established in every city, and of the choice of a superintendent and concerning his duties.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the placing a bishop or archbishop in his chair after ordination, which is enthronization.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: No one is allowed to transfer himself to another church [i.e., diocese] than that in which he was ordained; and what is to be done in the case of one cast out forcibly without any blame attaching to him.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: The laity shall not choose for themselves priests in the towns and villages without the authority of the chorepiscopus; nor an abbot for a monastery; and that no one should give commands as to who should be elected his successor after his death, and when this is lawful for a superior.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: How sisters, widows, and deaconesses should be made to keep their residence in their monasteries; and of the system of instructing them; and of the election of deaconesses, and of their duties and utility.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: How one seeking election should not be chosen, even if of conspicuous virtue; and how the election of a layman to the aforesaid grades is not prohibited, and that those chosen should not afterward be deprived before their deaths, except on account of crime.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the distinctive garb and distinctive names and conversation of monks and nuns.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: That a bishop convicted of adultery or of other similar crime should be deposed without hope of restoration to the same grade; but shall not be excommunicated.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of presbyters and deacons who have fallen only once into adultery, if they have never been married; and of the same when fallen as widowers, and those who have fallen, all the while having their own wives.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Also of those who return to the same sin as well widowers as those having living wives; and which of these ought not to be received to penance, and which once only, and which twice.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Each one of the faithful while his sin is yet not public should be mended by private exhortation and admonition; if he will not profit by this, he must be excommunicated.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the election of a procurator of the poor, and of his duties.
